papers.title: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
papers.summary: An LLM that learns, by self-supervision, when and how to call external APIs (calculator, search, translation, calendar) and merges the result back into its own generation — without losing general language ability.

insurance_business_case.headline: Lets a single underwriting / claims copilot orchestrate the patchwork of legacy insurance systems without per-system prompt engineering.
points: Underwriter copilot can call MIB, Rx, MVR, policy admin, and reinsurance pricing APIs as 'tools' from one chat surface.
Claims adjuster bot can self-decide when to look up benefit schedules, ICD-10 codes, or provider network rates instead of hard-coded workflows.
Reduces the integration backlog for AI assistants: new APIs become a tool registration, not a model retraining.

technical_details.pattern: Tool-augmented LLM via MCP (Model Context Protocol) — each insurance system exposed as an MCP tool, with the LLM selecting tools at inference time.
points: Wrap policy-admin (Ingenium / OIPA), claims (Guidewire ClaimCenter), CRM (Salesforce FSC) as MCP servers with typed schemas.
Govern tool selection with an allow-list + RBAC mapped to the underwriter/adjuster's AD group.
Log every tool call to a tamper-evident audit store (Azure Monitor + immutable blob) for FSRA / NAIC Model Bulletin 23-1 traceability.
Evaluate with replay harness on historical cases before promoting to production.

papers.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
papers.summary: Interleaves chain-of-thought reasoning with concrete actions (search, lookup) so an LLM can plan, observe, and revise — the foundational pattern behind today's agent frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, OpenAI Agents SDK).

insurance_business_case.headline: Production pattern for any multi-step insurance workflow that needs explainability (why did the agent ask for an APS?).
points: STP underwriting: agent reasons over application → decides which evidence to pull → re-evaluates after each piece arrives.
FNOL triage: agent reads the loss notice, decides whether to dispatch an adjuster, request medical records, or auto-approve under the small-claims threshold.
Each reasoning trace becomes the audit narrative regulators and complaint handlers can read in plain English.

technical_details.pattern: ReAct loop running on LangGraph / OpenAI Agents SDK, with persisted reasoning traces.
points: State graph: Observe (event) → Think (LLM CoT) → Act (tool) → Observe → ... → Decide.
Store every (thought, action, observation) tuple in an event-sourced store (e.g., Cosmos DB) keyed by case ID.
Add a guardrail node that blocks actions outside policy (e.g., 'never bind coverage above $1M without human').
Replay store powers QA sampling, complaint defence, and model regression tests.

papers.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
papers.summary: Trains an assistant to be helpful and harmless using a written 'constitution' of principles plus self-critique — replacing most human red-teaming with AI feedback.

insurance_business_case.headline: Direct template for a regulator-defensible 'AI Conduct Policy' your customer-facing bots must follow.
points: Encode TCF (Treating Customers Fairly), unfair-discrimination prohibitions, and CASL/CAN-SPAM rules as the constitution.
Self-critique catches mis-selling, off-label advice, and PII over-disclosure before a human ever sees it.
Gives the Chief Compliance Officer a single document mapping every model behaviour to a written principle.

technical_details.pattern: Constitutional layer wrapping the production LLM, plus a second 'critic' model trained on the constitution.
points: Author the constitution as YAML (principle id, source regulation, test prompts).
Run every customer response through critic → if score < threshold, regenerate with critique appended.
Nightly RLAIF fine-tune on disagreement cases to drift toward principle compliance.
Dashboards: per-principle violation rate, sliced by line of business and channel.

papers.title: Self-Consistency Improves Chain of Thought Reasoning in Language Models
papers.summary: Samples multiple reasoning paths and majority-votes the answer, dramatically improving accuracy on arithmetic and commonsense tasks without any retraining.

insurance_business_case.headline: Cheap accuracy boost for any numeric or rule-heavy insurance task — premium quotes, mortality factors, lapse probabilities.
points: Improves first-time-right rate on illustrations and quotes, reducing rework with distributors.
Cuts disputes on commission calculations by cross-checking the same prompt across N samples.
Detects model uncertainty: low-consensus answers automatically route to a human.

technical_details.pattern: Ensemble-of-thoughts inference layer over a single base model.
points: Run N (5–10) sampled completions at temperature 0.7; majority-vote the final structured answer.
Surface variance as a confidence score; route < threshold to human queue.
Cost-cap with adaptive N (only sample more when first 3 disagree).

papers.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
papers.summary: The original RAG paper — combines a parametric LLM with a non-parametric vector index so generation is grounded in retrievable documents.

insurance_business_case.headline: The backbone for every 'ask your policy/contract/regulation' assistant the business is asking for.
points: Producer copilot answers product questions from the current Product Guide, not stale training data.
Compliance officer queries the entire OSFI / FSRA / FINRA library and gets cited paragraphs.
Claims adjuster gets the exact policy clause that controls a benefit decision.

technical_details.pattern: Hybrid RAG (BM25 + dense vectors) with strict citation enforcement.
points: Index: Azure AI Search hybrid index over policy contracts, regulations, internal procedures.
Chunking: semantic chunking respecting clause boundaries; metadata = product, jurisdiction, effective date.
Prompt forces inline citations; responses without citations are rejected by post-filter.
Eval harness: 500-question gold set per LOB, refreshed quarterly.
